2024-12-20.thesis: Trump's mass-deportation executive order expands ICE detention capacity.

Trump's mass-deportation executive order remains unissued — inauguration Jan 20, 2025, is a fixed date with explicit campaign promises tied to it. Silence this week changes nothing; the catalyst is still ahead with a scheduled anchor. GEO remains the cleanest pure-play vehicle as the largest ICE detention contractor. Hold through quiet stretches; the actual signing is what matters.
2024-12-20.exit_advice: The order hasn't been signed — inauguration is the concrete trigger. EVICT ONLY on the signed order or an explicit public reversal of the deportation pledge. Do NOT exit on silence, stock drift, or 'already priced in' — the policy event itself hasn't occurred yet.

While the broader nuclear-for-data-centers theme may generate future catalysts (additional contracts, regulatory milestones), this particular event is complete. 2025-01-10.thesis: Trump's mass-deportation executive order expands ICE detention capacity.

No fresh coverage this week, but a quiet stretch ahead of a fixed calendar date is exactly when you HOLD. The EO signing is the binary event.
2025-01-10.exit_advice: The EO has not been signed and inauguration is still 10 days away. This is not staleness — it is the final stretch before the concrete date. 2025-01-17.thesis: Trump's mass-deportation executive order expands ICE detention capacity.
2025-01-17.assessment: Week ending Jan 17 — inauguration is Jan 20, three days away. This is the most proximate we've been to the concrete, dated catalyst since entry. No fresh news this week, but that's irrelevant: the event is 72 hours out. Per the fixed test, the catalyst has a concrete scheduled date and has NOT yet occurred. HOLD is the only coherent stance — exiting on silence three days before the binary date is exactly the hold-inertia trap in reverse (callous exit-inertia). GEO remains the cleanest pure-play vehicle as the leading ICE detention contractor.
2025-01-17.exit_advice: The EO signing on Jan 20 is the binary event; review the actual order's content (detention expansion scope, contract language) when it drops — that determines whether GEO's growth story accelerates or disappoints. Do not exit before the signing; do not assume the post-signing reaction.

2025-01-24.thesis: Trump's mass-deportation executive order expands ICE detention capacity.

2025-01-24.assessment: The concrete inauguration date of Jan 20 has passed, with Trump signing a barrage of executive orders, but none of the weekly news specifically mention the mass-deportation executive order expanding ICE detention capacity. Market coverage focuses on space and quantum stocks. The catalyst remains pending but is now post-scheduled date, increasing uncertainty and potentially delaying the thesis.
2025-01-24.exit_advice: The decisive catalyst was the EO signing at inauguration; its absence in recent news suggests possible delay or de-prioritization. Monitor closely for any announcement directly tying Trump's orders to ICE detention expansion. If no concrete news emerges in the next 1-2 weeks, exit as the edge erodes and the event may be priced into broader Trump-policy optimism.

2025-01-31.thesis: Trump's mass-deportation executive order expands ICE detention capacity.
2025-01-31.exit_case: Trump signed immigration enforcement executive orders on Jan 20 expanding detention — catalyst resolved and public.
2025-01-31.assessment: My Jan 24 note hedged because the weekly clips were garbage (space stocks, quantum stocks) and didn't cover what actually happened. Trump signed a slate of immigration enforcement executive orders on Inauguration Day (Jan 20), including directives to expand detention, end catch-and-release, and declare a border national emergency. The specific catalyst I entered on — a mass-deportation executive order expanding ICE detention capacity — has been signed. The catalyst resolved four days before my last note, and I failed to flag it because the news feed was irrelevant. The driving condition (ICE detention expansion via EO) has now occurred and is public. There is no further concrete scheduled step ahead. GEO has likely already reflected this on the post-election run. The event is done.
2025-01-31.exit_advice: EXIT. The executive orders were signed Jan 20. The binary event has landed, the thesis is fully public, and there is no remaining concrete catalyst ahead. Any further move in GEO will be driven by execution/earnings, not by this catalyst. Close the position.

2025-02-21.thesis: Microsoft's Majorana 1 chip unveiling re-rates pure-play quantum stocks
2025-02-21.exit_case: Majorana 1 already unveiled publicly and QBTS popped Thursday; but re-rating is in its earliest stage with no evidence of resolution yet.
2025-02-21.assessment: First week: Microsoft's Majorana 1 chip has been unveiled by Satya Nadella and is fully public. QBTS and peers (RGTI, QUBT) surged Thursday, initiating the re-rating. Coverage spans Yahoo Finance, Forbes, Computer Weekly, and Observer — broad tech press pickup but not yet retail-frenzy-level saturation. The chip itself is still theoretical (no commercial product yet), so the debate over timeline relevance is active: Nvidia's Jensen Huang said quantum is 'decades away,' and the Majorana 1 directly challenges that framing. The re-rating catalyst — Microsoft validating quantum's nearer-term viability — is freshly ignited, not exhausted. QBTS remains a clean pure-play vehicle among the movers. Key risk: quantum names are volatile/hype-prone and the initial two-day pop may create short-term pullback. The D-Wave article explicitly ties QBTS's Thursday move to the Majorana 1 news, confirming direct causal link. Far too early to assess whether re-rating has legs or fades; need to watch follow-on coverage (sell-side notes, institutional commentary) in coming weeks.
2025-02-21.exit_advice: HOLD. The unveiling fired and the initial pop is real, but the re-rating arc — sell-side quantum coverage resuming, institutional rotation into pure-plays, potential follow-on catalysts (government quantum funding announcements, other big-tech quantum reveals) — is week one. This is not stale; it is embryonic. Watch for: (1) QBTS holding gains or consolidating vs. fading back to pre-announcement levels, (2) meaningful sell-side initiation/price-target activity on quantum names, (3) any narrower quantum catalysts (government contract, partnership) that could provide a second leg. If QBTS fades entirely within 1-2 weeks back to pre-event levels with no follow-on coverage, the re-rating is dead — exit then.

2025-02-28.thesis: Microsoft's Majorana 1 chip unveiling re-rates pure-play quantum stocks
2025-02-28.exit_case: No quantum coverage this week — quiet stretch, NOT a resolution/reversal; driving condition intact, still early.
2025-02-28.assessment: One week in, zero quantum-related coverage this week — the news feed was quiet for QBTS. That said, per instructions, a quiet stretch is not an exit condition. The driving condition — Microsoft validating quantum's nearer-term viability via Majorana 1, directly challenging Jensen Huang's 'decades away' framing — remains intact and un-debunked. No sell-side notes or institutional follow-through are yet visible, but that just means the re-rating arc is in its early, still-unfolding stage. The initial two-day pop may create near-term choppiness, but the thesis was never a one-day trade; it's a re-rating event that needs sell-side attention and institutional repositioning to play out. The under-the-radar quality is actually intact — if anything, the silence suggests we're still early, not late. QBTS remains the cleanest pure-play pure-quantum vehicle. Holding through the quiet.
2025-02-28.exit_advice: The Majorana 1 unveiling and its re-rating of quantum names is still in week one of its reaction cycle. No follow-on sell-side coverage or institutional commentary has emerged yet, but the driving condition (Microsoft validation of quantum timeline) is live and un-debunked. Silences like this week — with zero quantum news — are expected in early re-rating arcs before sell-side notes and follow-ons arrive. The smart-money window is still wide open; no concrete next catalyst is scheduled but the arc is nowhere near complete. QBTS remains the preferred vehicle. Continue holding; the moment to reassess is if quantum hype narratives flip bearish or if sell-side follow-through actually arrives and is fully absorbed.
